##NAMES=Rruffite
##RRUFFID=R141211
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_2_Cu(AsO_4_)_2_·2H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Jote mine, Pampa Larga District, Tierra Amarilla, Copiapo Province, Chile
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Bob Jenkins
##DESCRIPTION=Pale-blue to white euhedral crystals, associated with light-pinkish mansfieldite, green conichalcite, and blue lavedulan
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R141211
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=Ca_1.98_(Cu_0.97_Zn_0.03_)(As_1.06_O_4_)_2_·2H_2_O ; trace Mn, Mg
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 5.866 b: 12.78 c: 5.705 alpha: 90 beta: 109.5 gamma: 90 volume: 403.12 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
